blocks.body: Negative questions are questions with a negative form that usually express surprise, surprise, or the expectation of a positive answer. They can sound like criticism or sarcasm, so it is important to understand the context of their use.

{ "title": "10. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "Negative questions вЂ” questions with a negative form of the auxiliary verb.", "Structure: Don't/Doesn't/Didn't + subject + verb / Isn't/Aren't + subject.", "Meaning: surprise, expectation of a positive response, polite request or criticism.", "With do/does/did: Don't you know? / Doesn't she work here? / Didn't they come?", "With to be: Isn't she happy? / Aren't you ready? / Wasn't it good?", "With modals: Can't you swim? / Won't she come? Shouldn't we go? вЂ” Yes, I do (I love).", "Р’С–РґРїРѕРІС–РґСЊ: Yes/No Р·Р° Р·РјС–СЃС‚РѕРј, РЅРµ Р·Р° С„РѕСЂРјРѕСЋ РїРёС‚Р°РЅРЅСЏ.", "Yes = Р·РіРѕРґР° Р· РїРѕР·РёС‚РёРІРѕРј: Don't you like it? вЂ” Yes, I do (СЏ Р»СЋР±Р»СЋ).", "No = agreement with the negative: Don't you like it? вЂ” No, I don't (I don't like).", "Context is important: intonation determines whether it is surprise, criticism or politeness." ] }
